name: Ray Wetzel
abstract: Ray Wetzel (September 22, 1924 – August 17, 1951) was an American jazz trumpeter. Critic Scott Yanow described him as "greatly admired by his fellow trumpeters".

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: The '49 band, the trumpet section was Johnny Howell, Ray Wetzel, Maynard Ferguson, Doc Severinsen and Ralph Ericson.

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Vax
about_quotes.evidence: Ray just — they were just going to use it as a break tune so Ray just taught it to everybody.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Vax interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Igoe
about_quotes.evidence: He says, 'did you ever hear of Ray Wetzel?' I said, 'sure, the great trumpet player.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Sonny Igoe interviewed by Monk Rowe, Emerson, New Jersey, July 31, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: And she was Bonnie Wetzel. She was Ray Wetzel’s divorced wife.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 9, 2013
